tiles.title: Avg Dependents by Loan_ID
tiles.insight: The majority of loan IDs have an average of 2 dependents, with no significant differences between them. This suggests that the number of dependents is relatively consistent across different loan IDs, which may not be a key factor in loan decisions.

tiles.title: Dependents distribution
tiles.insight:
Most Dependents values in our financial data are zero, making up 63% of all records. A small number of higher values, however, account for the remaining 37% of the data, indicating a skewed distribution. 
So what: This suggests that our financial data may not be representative of households with multiple dependents.

tiles.title: Loan_ID breakdown
tiles.insight: Most loan IDs are spread across 8 different groups, with no single group making up more than 1% of the records. This balanced distribution suggests that the loan data is evenly distributed across various groups, which can help with data analysis and modeling.

tiles.title: Correlation with Dependents
tiles.insight: LoanAmount is weakly and positively linked to Dependents, but this relationship is relatively weak and doesn't tell us much about how these two factors interact. So what, this suggests that Dependents and LoanAmount are largely independent factors in loan decisions.
